preamble: The transformation of the voiceless glottal fricative /h/ to the alveolar nasal /n/ is an attested sound change, particularly observed in certain Algonquian languages. This shift involves a significant alteration in both the place and manner of articulation, as well as voicing.
phoneticEffects: This shift entails a change from a voiceless glottal fricative /h/ to a voiced alveolar nasal /n/
sources: MacKenzie, M. E. (1980). Towards a Dialectology of Cree-Montagnais-Naskapi (Doctoral dissertation). McGill University.
